items.title: Maybank aware of Indonesian authorities' inquiries involving corporate customers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malayan Banking Bhd (Maybank) has confirmed it is aware of reports that Indonesian authorities have made enquiries in relation to an ongoing matter involving certain corporate customers of the group. In a statement, the bank said personnel from Maybank Indonesia have been asked to provide information as witnesses to assist the authorities. "Maybank Indonesia's personnel has been requested to provide information as witnesses to assist the authorities, and the bank is not the subject of any investigation," it said.

items.title: Sabah Wildlife Dept seizes 300 turtle eggs in Tuaran, arrest man
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 10 – A joint special operation conducted by the Wildlife Crime Bureau (WCB) and the Sabah Wildlife Department has led to the arrest of a 37-year-old Malaysian man in Kampung Serusup, Tuaran, about 40km from here for alleged possession of what is believed to be protected turtle eggs. The operation seized approximately 300 eggs believed to be from green turtles, a protected species in Sabah. The Sabah Wildlife Department emphasised that it takes any form of illegal possession, trade, or smuggling of wildlife very seriously.

items.title: 'Ustaz Budak' loses appeal in boy assault case, faces 12 more charges
items.summary: Judicial Commissioner Azhar Mokhtar imposed the sentence after dismissing an appeal by Syamsul Zaman Sukri against his conviction and sentence handed down by the Pasir Mas Sessions Court. Syamsul Zaman, a pondok school principal and independent preacher, was first charged at the Pasir Mas Sessions Court in August 2021. Syamsul Zaman was subsequently found guilty by the Sessions Court before filing an appeal to the High Court.

items.title: 'Bersatu has no value in PN': 13 MPs tell party to leave coalition
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thirteen Bersatu members of parliament have called on the party to leave Perikatan Nasional (PN), following Pas' decision to sever ties with it. The MPs said Pas' move was unsurprising, attributing it to the "arrogance" of Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, secretary-general Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali and the party's top leadership. "Bersatu no longer has any value in PN.

items.title: Cops launch manhunt for suspect after fatal stabbing in Ipoh
items.summary: Perak Tengah district police chief Supt Hafezul Helmi Hamzah said police received information about the incident at about 5.49am. He said further investigations revealed that the suspect fled the scene in a black Proton Wira bearing registration number WGX 3707. Information can also be channelled to any nearby police station,” he said.

items.title: Gold bullion forfeiture challenge linked to ex-army chief’s wife set for July 31
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The Sessions Court here today allowed the government’s application to issue a third-party notice in the Gazette against anyone wishing to challenge the forfeiture of 3.85 kg of gold bullion seized from an acquaintance of Salwani Anuar @ Kamaruddin, the wife of former Army Chief Tan Sri Muhammad Hafizuddeain Jantan. Judge Suzana Hussin set July 31 as the hearing date for any party with an interest in the gold bullion, which was seized from contractor Mohamad Amirul Yusrie Aminorddin @ Ab Kadir, 31. Also seized were jewellery worth RM294,039, two diamond rings valued at RM20,000, 3.7 kg of gold bullion worth RM2.5 million, a 150-g gold medallion worth RM102,000, and two gold dinars weighing 8.5 g valued at RM5,780.

items.title: Malaysia records 17.5 million tourist arrivals by May as VM2026 campaign gains momentum
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 10 — Foreign visitor arrivals under the Visit Malaysia 2026 (VM2026) campaign are on a strong trajectory, with 17.5 million tourists recorded as of May, says Deputy Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Chiew Choon Man. He said that in pursuit of the campaign’s target of 47 million visitors, the ministry, through Tourism Malaysia, had organised various promotional programmes both domestically and abroad to attract more tourists to Malaysia. Meanwhile, Chiew today launched the special registration number series “V 1 MY” to “V 9999 MY” in conjunction with VM2026.

items.title: Underground gas leak halts drilling at Northern Coastal Highway site
items.summary: KUCHING: An underground gas leak was detected during drilling works at the Northern Coastal Highway project site in Kampung Datuk, Kuala Lawas, in northern Sarawak, today. The Lawas Fire and Rescue Station was notified of the incident at 12.33pm after workers detected gas leaking from the ground surface. A Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department operations centre spokesman said the team arrived at the location at 12.50pm after travelling 18km.

items.title: PTPTN allocates RM60.1m in advance loans to help 40,099 new IPTA, polytechnic students
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The National Higher Education Fund Corporation (PTPTN) has allocated RM60.1 million in Loan Advance Payment (WPP) assistance to 40,099 new diploma students enrolling at public higher education institutions (IPTAs) and polytechnics for the June 2026 intake. PTPTN said each eligible student would receive RM1,500 through the WPP to help cover part of the initial expenses associated with enrolling in higher education institutions, thereby easing the financial burden on their families. It added that the WPP is offered to Malaysians who agree to receive the facility when applying for admission to public higher education institutions through UPUOnline or the respective public university admission portals.

items.title: Sabah police bust 12 scam call centres, arrest 341 suspects
items.summary: Sabah Police Commissioner Datuk Jauteh Dikun said the special operation was carried out by the State Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) following continuous intelligence gathering and monitoring. He said the raids were conducted in Kota Kinabalu, Labuan, Tawau, Sandakan and Penampang. Jauteh said investigations revealed that the suspects were assigned various roles within the syndicates, including operations supervisors, customer service officers, system programmers, project managers, financial administrators, and personnel directly contacting victims.

items.title: Zambry: Malaysia open to Hong Kong university ties
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia is open to exploring higher education collaborations with the University of Hong Kong and other international institutions as part of efforts to strengthen academic cooperation and global engagement, Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said today. "We can also discuss the possibility of having collaborations in the field of higher education with Professor Li Cheng, who represents Hong Kong University," he said after attending Li's lecture titled China-US Relations and Impact to Malaysia at Dewan Za'ba, the Higher Education Ministry. I hope that Malaysia, Hong Kong, mainland China and many other countries can benefit from education.

items.title: High Court in KL upholds RM60,000 award against man who shared ex-girlfriend’s intimate photos online
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 10 — The Kuala Lumpur High Court today upheld a Tribunal for Anti-Sexual Harassment (TAGS) award in favour of a woman whose ex-boyfriend shared her intimate photos online without her consent. “This decision by the Kuala Lumpur High Court reinforces TAGS’ role as a fast, simple and effective justice mechanism in providing remedies to victims of sexual harassment, including digital sexual harassment,” the statement read. KPWKM said TAGS, established on March 8, 2024, provides an alternative channel to civil courts for hearing and deciding sexual harassment complaints.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 10, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed higher today, as investors shifted into defensive and domestic-focused stocks amid weaker global tech shares and rising US-Iran tensions. Gains in banking heavyweights helped lift the benchmark index. That wraps up today's Market Pulse.

items.title: Lift all UN child rights reservations, govt told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Bar has called for the government and political parties to remove all reservations to the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child. Its president Anand Raj said that although Malaysia has seen positive developments in reducing the number of reservations in the past years, he said that such reservations should not be there in the first place. "The Malaysian Bar calls upon all political parties and the Malaysian government to commit to removing all these reservations forthwith to bring us in line with the practice of all civilised nations.

items.title: Manhunt launched after fatal stabbing in Perak
items.summary: PASIR SALAK: Police are hunting a suspect believed to have fatally stabbed a 29-year-old man during a fight at Km 12, Jalan Trans Perak, early today. Perak Tengah district police chief Superintendent Hafezul Helmi Hamzah said they received information on the incident at 5.49am. He said initial investigations found the victim suffered stab wounds during an altercation with a 30-year-old man who has been identified by police.

items.title: Ringgit eases against greenback ahead of US CPI release
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended lower against the US dollar today as market sentiment remained cautious ahead of the release of the United States' May Consumer Price Index (CPI) data. Regional developments, especially the surprise interest rate hike by the Bank of Indonesia (on Tuesday), somehow affected market sentiment, with the strong US labour market portending an interest rate hike by the Fed. However, Mohd Afzanizam said, the latest BNM's international reserves of US$130.6 billion suggests demand for the ringgit remained healthy.

items.title: AirBorneo apologises for Sabah, Sarawak flight disruptions, commits to service recovery
items.summary: KUCHING, June 10 — AirBorneo has apologised to guests affected by recent flight delays and cancellations across Sarawak and Sabah, acknowledging the disruption, inconvenience and uncertainty experienced by travellers and their families over the past few days. AirBorneo chief executive officer Megat Ardian Wira Mohd Aminuddin said the airline understands the frustration, inconvenience and uncertainty this has caused. Megat Ardian Wira said AirBorneo has implemented additional operational and maintenance measures to strengthen fleet resilience and support the progressive restoration of normal services.

items.title: Anwar: Japan visit strengthens investment, AI and trade ties
items.summary: He said Malaysia highlighted several key priorities in the meeting with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, including high-value investments, new technologies and cooperation in the field of artificial intelligence (AI). “We prioritise this because we need cooperation and support not only from the Japanese government but also from major Japanese companies,” he said at the final press conference of the three-day official visit that began on Monday. The visit was also used to hold meetings with major Japanese companies, including Mitsubishi Corporation, ENEOS Corporation, DENSO Corporation, and Marubeni Corporation, to explore opportunities for expanding cooperation and investment in Malaysia.

items.title: 20 years of neglect: Span cracks down on factories over untreated sewage risk
items.summary: IPOH: The National Water Services Commission (Span) has uncovered cases of industrial premises failing to desludge and maintain their septic tanks for as long as 20 years. Span Operations Division Enforcement Section director Ainal Yusman Mohamad Yusop said some premises had failed to carry out desludging beyond the mandatory two-year cycle, with the worst case involving a premise that had not undertaken the maintenance process for two decades. Under existing procedures, IWK issues notices to premises owners requiring mandatory septic tank desludging every two years.

items.title: Malaysia's top brands hit US$62.2bil as Petronas keeps top spot, Genting surges to No. 2
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's top 100 brands grew in value by five per cent year-on-year to US$62.2 billion in 2026, according to the Brand Finance Malaysia 100 2026 report. Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) retained its position as Malaysia's most valuable brand for the 16th consecutive year, with brand value falling four per cent to US$13.8 billion. Malayan Banking Bhd (Maybank) maintained third position, with brand value rising four per cent to US$5.4 billion.

items.title: Police step up anti-gambling crackdown ahead of World Cup
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police will intensify nationwide enforcement to curb illegal gambling, particularly sports betting and online gambling, ahead of the World Cup 2026, which begins tomorrow. "Among the areas of focus are monitoring and enforcement actions against online gambling and betting activities involving online gambling syndicates, platforms conducting gambling and betting operations, as well as promotions carried out through social media accounts. "Action will also be taken against physical gambling and betting activities involving illegal gambling and betting premises, individuals placing bets, betting agents, and premises or individuals promoting gambling and betting in physical form, he said in a statement.

items.title: Perlis govt carries on as usual amid Perikatan leadership uncertainty, says Shahidan Kassim
items.summary: KANGAR, June 10 — The Perlis state government will continue to function as usual pending a decision by the Perikatan Nasional (PN) leadership following PAS’ move to end its political cooperation with Bersatu. Perlis PN chairman and PAS Perlis deputy commissioner III, Datuk Seri Dr Shahidan Kassim, said efforts to safeguard the welfare of the people would remain the coalition’s priority despite the political uncertainty. The Perlis state assembly currently comprises six PAS assemblymen, five from Bersatu, and one Pakatan Harapan assemblyman.

items.title: Bank of America uses World Cup sponsorship to expand connectivity push
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank of America is using its first FIFA sponsorship to strengthen engagement with corporate clients and highlight its ability to support cross-border business activities, as companies navigate an increasingly interconnected global economy. The bank's Malaysia country executive Gautam Puntambekar said its sponsorship of the FIFA Club World Cup 2025 and FIFA World Cup 2026 provides a multi-year global platform that differentiates it from other financial institutions. He said the partnership aligns with FIFA's worldwide audience and reflects the bank's international presence across markets.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain expected across nine states and Labuan, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit nine states and Labuan until 7pm tonight, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). In an alert issued at 5pm, MetMalaysia said two northern states are expected to experience these weather conditions: in Kedah, the affected areas are Kuala Muda, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu; while in Penang, they cover Seberang Perai Utara, Seberang Perai Tengah and Seberang Perai Selatan. In Perak, the forecast covers the coastal areas of Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Manjung, and Hilir Perak, as well as inland areas of Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim.

items.title: Trump setbacks fuel lame-duck talk as he turns 80
items.summary: Still, Trump has shown he retains significant clout: he has helped oust Republican incumbents in primary races and has pressed ahead with aggressive trade policies. This dynamic is unfolding just months ahead of November's midterm elections as Trump's Republican Party scrambles to maintain control of Congress. White House spokeswoman Olivia Wales said, "President Trump is the unequivocal leader of the Republican Party who is committed to maintaining Republicans' majority in Congress."

items.title: Govt’s RON95, diesel subsidy bill estimated to hit RM3.5b a month, says Finance Ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The government’s subsidy bill for RON95 petrol and diesel is projected to remain high at about RM3.5 billion a month at current market prices, amid the ongoing conflict in West Asia, the Ministry of Finance (MOF) said. Of the total, subsidies for RON95 petrol are estimated at RM2 billion a month, while diesel subsidies are projected at around RM1.5 billion a month, the ministry said. At the same time, the MADANI government continues to strengthen long-term energy security measures and targeted subsidy initiatives as the global supply crisis persists, the ministry said.

items.title: Bookies beware: Bukit Aman launches Op Soga XI ahead of Fifa World Cup 2026 betting surge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) has launched Op Soga XI nationwide to intensify enforcement against illegal gambling activities, particularly sports betting and online gambling expected to rise in conjunction with the Fifa World Cup 2026. The operation targets both online and physical gambling activities, including syndicate-run betting platforms, websites and social media accounts promoting illegal wagering. It also covers physical gambling activities such as illegal betting premises, bookmakers, agents, and individuals involved in organising or promoting gambling activities.

items.title: Cermat Madani supports push for more affordable takaful, insurance: MTA
items.summary: Malaysian Takaful Association (MTA) said the initiative reflects ongoing efforts by regulators and policymakers to reform the insurance ecosystem including motor coverage by gradually moving towards risk-based pricing mechanisms. MTA chief executive officer Mohd Redzuan Mohamed said the programme's emphasis on driving behaviour, traffic summons records and the potential future use of telematics data supports the industry's long-term shift towards risk-based pricing models. Implemented in collaboration with 10 insurance and takaful providers, the initiative uses driving records and traffic summons data through the MyJPJ application to assess motorists' risk profiles.

items.title: Police seize RM110,000 ecstasy, arrest labourer in Kluang
items.summary: KLUANG: A labourer with eight previous criminal and drug-related offences faces the death penalty after police seized more than 1kg of ecstasy worth RM110,000 in a midnight operation in Simpang Renggam yesterday. The man was arrested during routine crime prevention patrols along Jalan Pasar, Simpang Renggam, at 12.15am. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Bahrin Mohd Noh said 13 packets containing 1,116.8g of ecstasy were found on the suspect.

items.title: Azam Baki says 'truth prevailed' after probe is closed
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief Tan Sri Azam Baki welcomed a decision by the Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC) to classify a criminal intimidation investigation against him as no further action (NFA). Azam's remarks came after the A-GC classified a police investigation paper involving an alleged criminal intimidation complaint against him as NFA . "I am grateful that the truth has finally prevailed," Azam said in a text message to NST.

items.title: RM1,500 cash advance: PTPTN rolls out RM60m aid for students
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The National Higher Education Fund Corporation (PTPTN) has allocated RM60.1 million in Loan Advance Payment (WPP) assistance to 40,099 new diploma students enrolling in public universities and polytechnics for the June 2026 intake. PTPTN said each eligible student would receive RM1,500 to help cover initial enrolment expenses and ease the financial burden on their families. The corporation said the assistance is available to Malaysian students who agreed to receive the facility when applying for admission through UPUOnline or their respective public university admission portals.

items.title: Stratus Global signs underwriting agreement with UOB Kay Hian ahead of Main Market listing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Stratus Global Holdings Bhd has signed an underwriting agreement with UOB Kay Hian (M) Sdn Bhd ahead of its initial public offering (IPO) and listing on the Main Market. Stratus Global is a semiconductor automated material handling system (AMHS) specialist providing automation solutions for the movement and storage of materials used in chip manufacturing. The company serves customers in Malaysia and key semiconductor markets globally.

items.title: BN's Johor, Negri election line-up almost set
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Barisan Nasional (BN) is on track to finalise candidates for the Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections, said its secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir. He said preliminary discussions had already been held, with candidate selection and seat allocations among component parties now the main focus. On the possibility of overlapping claims among component parties, he said BN's culture of consensus would prevail.

items.title: Sabah wildlife wardens undergo vetting before appointment, says minister
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: State Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said honorary wildlife wardens in Sabah undergo strict screening and training before appointment. He said all appointees undergo a structured process, including interviews, vetting and field-based training in enforcement-related duties. Jafry said the inclusion of community members as wardens — numbering about 1,247 statewide — had fostered a sense of ownership and responsibility among locals in safeguarding Sabah's wildlife and natural heritage.

items.title: RTM mobilises resources for World Cup coverage as RTM Klik hits seven million downloads
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) is committed to delivering comprehensive coverage of all 104 matches of the FIFA World Cup 2026 across its television and digital platforms. Director-General of Broadcasting Ashwad Ismail said football fans nationwide will be able to follow the tournament through RTM Klik and RTM channels on MYTV and Unifi TV. He said RTM is mobilising its full resources to ensure the quadrennial tournament is accessible to all Malaysians, including those in rural areas.

items.title: Amnesty accuses Israel of 'ethnic cleansing' of West Bank Bedouins
items.summary: RAMALLAH: Amnesty International accused Israel on Wednesday of conducting an "ethnic cleansing" campaign against Bedouin and herding communities in the occupied West Bank, saying the measures were designed to accelerate the annexation of the Palestinian territory. "Israeli authorities are accelerating annexation through a state-driven campaign of ethnic cleansing targeting Palestinian Bedouin and herding communities" of the West Bank, said the report released on Wednesday. In the report titled "Erasing anything Palestinian: Israel's ethnic cleansing of West Bank Bedouin and herding communities", Amnesty accused Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's government, one of Israel's most right-wing to date, of catering to the settler movement's religious nationalist agenda.

items.title: Zahid: Malaysian envoys play key role in strengthening bilateral ties abroad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Malaysian heads of mission representing the country abroad shoulder a significant responsibility in strengthening bilateral ties and upholding the nation’s image on the international stage, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said. In a Facebook post today, Ahmad Zahid said he was confident that the experience and expertise of each diplomat would enable them to carry out their duties effectively. Ahmad Zahid made the remarks after receiving a courtesy call from 13 newly appointed Malaysian heads of mission before they departed to assume their postings abroad.

items.title: CAAM monitoring AirBorneo disruptions amid delays and cancellations in Sabah, Sarawak
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 10 — The Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM) is closely monitoring recent flight delays, cancellations and schedule changes affecting AirBorneo services across Sabah and Sarawak. CAAM said the airline had attributed the disruptions to operational and maintenance-related issues. While stressing that safety must remain the highest priority in flight operations, the authority reminded AirBorneo that affected consumers must continue to be treated fairly and in accordance with the Malaysian Aviation Consumer Protection Code 2016 (MACPC).

items.title: Wawasan QI's Kami Builders raises RM90mil via SRI sukuk
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Wawasan QI Properties Sdn Bhd's unit Kami Builders Sdn Bhd has issued RM90 million in Tranche 1 SRI sukuk murabahah. This is part of Kami Builders's RM300 million Asean Sustainability SRI sukuk programmes. The funds will be channelled into the development of a new university campus in Ipoh, Perak.

items.title: Belfast violence 'shocking and completely unacceptable': UK PM
items.summary: LONDON: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Wednesday slammed violence in Belfast following a brutal knife attack allegedly carried out by a Sudanese man as "shocking and completely unacceptable." Those responsible will feel the full force of the law," Starmer said in a statement on X. Anti-immigration protesters torched buildings and vehicles in Belfast on Tuesday evening and blocked roads, a day after the stabbing. Earlier, Northern Ireland police also appealed for calm after the stabbing.

items.title: Pas continues supporting Perlis govt despite Bersatu split
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pas will continue supporting the Perlis government for now, taking into account the decree of the Raja of Perlis. Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said the party's position was decided at a recent Pas Central Working Committee meeting, despite the party's decision to end its political cooperation with Bersatu. Bersatu yesterday said it was disappointed by Pas' decision to end political cooperation between the two parties.

items.title: Teen driver in fatal Kluang crash released on police bail
items.summary: MUAR: A teenager who allegedly drove recklessly and caused a crash that killed four family members in Kluang last week has been released on police bail pending prosecution. Kluang district police chief Assistant Commissioner Bahrin Mohd Noh said the deputy public prosecutor directed that the 19-year-old suspect be granted bail until investigations are fully completed. The two-day extension of the suspect's remand order, which began on Monday, expired today.

items.title: Non-Muslim religious council objects to Selangor worship-house guidelines, says 5,000-resident rule and commercial-lot restrictions unrealistic
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The Malaysian Consultative Council of Buddhism, Christianity, Hinduism, Sikhism and Taoism (MCCBCHST) has objected to parts of Selangor’s 2025 planning guidelines for non-Muslim houses of worship, saying several conditions for obtaining prayer spaces were too restrictive. MCCBCHST also objected to restrictions against placing non-Muslim houses of worship in commercial business zones, converting existing buildings into places of worship, and using residential houses for worship purposes. 34/2025 dated November 12, 2025,” the council said.

items.title: RTD seizes over 300 heavily modified motorcycles nationwide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Motorcycles with extreme modifications costing up to RM18,000 were among those flagged during enforcement operations conducted by the Road Transport Department (RTD) under Ops Ekstrem recently. RTD senior enforcement director Datuk Muhammad Kifli Ma Hassan said more than 300 motorcycles with illegal and extreme modifications had been seized nationwide, with most cases recorded in Kuala Lumpur. Also present was Kuala Lumpur RTD director Hamidi Adam.

items.title: FBI Southeast Asia scam centres: US agency targets Chinese-linked cyber fraud networks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The United States Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) is intensifying cooperation with Southeast Asian law enforcement agencies to dismantle scam compounds and transnational criminal syndicates operating across the region. FBI co-deputy director Andrew Bailey said scam centres in Southeast Asia had evolved into highly organised criminal enterprises responsible for stealing billions of dollars, trafficking people and exploiting emerging technologies, including cryptocurrency. Bailey said investigations had uncovered strong links between scam compounds operating throughout Southeast Asia and sophisticated criminal organisations based in China.

items.title: Suspect in murder of man found bound in mattress to be charged tomorrow
items.summary: MELAKA: The suspect in a murder case involving a decomposed body found wrapped in a rolled mattress with bound hands at a shop lot in Pokok Mangga here will be charged in court tomorrow. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the investigation papers have been completed and returned by the deputy public prosecutor's office. It was previously reported that the body of a man, believed to be a murder victim, was discovered inside a folded mattress with his hands tied at a shop lot in Pokok Mangga around 7pm on May 28.

items.title: Cabinet makes mall recycling facilities mandatory, full enforcement from 2028
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 10 — More than 1,000 shopping malls nationwide will be equipped with Recycling Facilities (FPS) by the end of this year following a Cabinet decision to make such facilities mandatory as part of efforts to strengthen solid waste management and accelerate the country’s transition towards a circular economy. “The move to make recycling facilities in shopping malls a licensing requirement will facilitate public convenience while strengthening the national sustainability agenda. KPKT aims to make recycling as easy as shopping.

items.title: Pahang Health Dept: Healthcare services uninterrupted despite doctor crunch
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Pahang Health Department has assured the public that healthcare services will remain uninterrupted despite a shortage of medical officers at certain facilities across the state. The department said that as of June 1, there were 850 medical officers serving at healthcare facilities under its jurisdiction. "The department is aware of complaints on social media concerning the shortage of medical officers in several hospitals across Pahang.

items.title: Finance Ministry leads push to stop illegal foreign goods and businesses and protect Malaysian jobs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The Ministry of Finance (MOF) is leading relevant ministries and agencies in adopting a “whole-of-government” approach to address the illegal influx of foreign products, services and businesses, said Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong. In a Facebook post today, Liew said the approach aims to ensure a level playing field for Malaysian companies and industries. On June 8, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim expressed concern over the growing number of foreigners who misuse visitor and student visas to engage in business activities in Malaysia.

items.title: Terengganu MB says exco line-up will not change amid PAS-Bersatu rift
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Terengganu Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar has reportedly said there will be no reshuffle of the state executive council despite PAS ending its political cooperation with Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia. His was reportedly responding to questions over the position of Terengganu Bersatu chief Datuk Razali Idris, who is currently a state executive councillor, after PAS decided to sever political ties with Bersatu. In the Terengganu state election in August 2023, Bersatu won five state seats: Hulu Besut, Permaisuri, Seberang Takir, Telemong and Kijal.

items.title: Economy minister says Malaysia looks at Russia, Türkiye as potential new oil supply sources
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 10 — The government is exploring new crude oil sources from several regions to diversify the country’s energy supply amid global geopolitical uncertainty, Economy Minister Datuk Seri Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said. “Each crude oil supply from different regions has different characteristics, so an assessment must be made on whether our facilities can receive and process the supply. He said countries currently being explored as potential new supply sources include Russia and Türkiye.

items.title: With 850 doctors across Pahang, Health Dept says it's moving staff from clinics to hospitals to fill gaps
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 10 — The Pahang State Health Department (JKNP) said the shortage of medical officers at several healthcare facilities in the state is due to factors such as the movement of doctors undergoing specialist training. JKNP, in a statement today, said the current number of medical officers at healthcare facilities under the department stands at 850 as of June 1. “JKNP takes note of complaints that went viral on social media regarding the shortage of medical officers at several hospitals in Pahang.

items.title: High Court orders return of Daim’s daughter’s passport, finds ‘no merit’ in holding it
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The High Court today granted the daughter of the late former Finance Minister Tun Daim Zainuddin permission to keep her international passport permanently. Judge Nurulhuda Nur’Aini Mohamad Nor made the ruling after hearing submissions from deputy public prosecutor Nidzuwan Abd Latip and lawyer Rajesh Nagarajan, who represented Asnida Abdul Daim, 65, during case management proceedings. The shareholdings involved five companies: Amiraz Sdn Bhd, Arida Farms Sdn Bhd, Kangkung Catering Sdn Bhd, Transgrade Sdn Bhd, and Gajah3 Foods Sdn Bhd.

items.title: Digitally active Malaysians have good financial literacy but security remains elusive for many, study finds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Digitally engaged Malaysians have relatively strong financial literacy, but many still struggle to turn that knowledge into long-term financial security, a new report has found. “Many respondents understand key financial concepts and are highly motivated to improve their financial futures, yet translating that knowledge into consistent financial behaviours and long-term financial security remains a challenge,” he said. The report analysed anonymised responses from 4,004 Versa platform users collected in December 2025, using the OECD/INFE Financial Literacy Framework to assess financial knowledge, behaviour, attitudes, digital financial engagement, financial well-being and retirement readiness.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan DAP to keep over half of its incumbents for state polls
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 10 — Negeri Sembilan DAP will retain more than half of its incumbent representatives as candidates in the upcoming state election as it seeks to maintain its impressive record of winning every seat it has contested since 2013. He said the party also intends to field several new faces in the election as it seeks to defend the 11 seats it won in the last state election. The 2023 Negeri Sembilan state election saw Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) form the state government after securing 31 of the 36 seats.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan Bersatu says it will contest election under PN banner despite PAS split
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Negeri Sembilan Bersatu has said it will continue to contest under the Perikatan Nasional (PN) banner in the next state election, despite PAS’ decision to end political cooperation with the party. PN currently holds five seats in the Negeri Sembilan state assembly, three through PAS and two through Bersatu. Meanwhile, Utusan Malaysia reported that Negeri Sembilan PAS commissioner Mohd Fairuz Mohd Isa reportedly said no decision had been made on whether PAS would use the PN logo or contest under the PN ticket in the next state election.

items.title: Malaysian banks honour 68 staff who helped save RM139m from scammers last year
items.summary: The banking personnel were feted at the second consecutive #JanganKenaScam Bank Heroes Recognition Event 2026, a nationwide anti-scam initiative organised by The Association of Banks in Malaysia (ABM) and The Association of Islamic Banking and Financial Institutions Malaysia (Aibim), in close collaboration with the police. The associations said the numbers reflect the aggressive scale of modern financial crime and the corresponding ramp-up in banking defences. National Taskforce to Combat Fraud chairman Syed Ahmad Taufik Albar speaks at the #JanganKenaScam Bank Heroes Recognition Event 2026 in Petaling Jaya.

items.title: JB Sentral closure: KTMB activates bus transfers amid RTS Link project works from June 11
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Train services at JB Sentral Station will be temporarily suspended starting tomorrow to facilitate the launching of the truss structure for the Johor Bahru–Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link Project. Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) in a statement today said that during the closure period, passengers involved in the ETS EP9535 and EG9449 trains will continue their journey using the intermediary bus service provided between Kempas Baru Station and JB Sentral. Affected passengers can contact the KTMB Call Centre at 03-9779 1200 for further information.

items.title: Sabah baker injured in home accident becomes early beneficiary of Socso’s 24-hour protection scheme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — A young man from Sabah who fell from a staircase at home has become one of the first beneficiaries under the Non-Work-Related Accident Scheme (SKBBK), or LINDUNG 24 Jam, which took effect on June 1. In a statement today, the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) said the victim, Arnold Nathanael Lister, 24, qualified for coverage as an active contributor, despite the accident happening outside working hours. Perkeso noted that previously, domestic accident victims often struggled to get financial protection, but the new scheme now covers treatment costs and safeguards the livelihoods of contributors.

items.title: PAS taps Annuar Musa to lead Johor election campaign
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 10 — PAS has appointed former federal minister Tan Sri Annuar Musa as its Johor election director ahead of the upcoming state polls. Johor PAS information chief Mohd Firdaus Jaffar said the appointment was aimed at strengthening the party’s preparations for the election. “PAS Johor welcomes Tan Sri Annuar Musa’s appointment as the party’s state election director.
